checks.finding: REQ-001 through REQ-005 define observable guards, fixed signature behavior, path-validation outcomes, deterministic policy exits, and generated-artifact controls mapped to TEST-001 through TEST-013.
Every Phase 2 goal has one or more named acceptance criteria: REQ-001 to AC-001/002, REQ-002 to AC-003/004, REQ-003 to AC-005/006, REQ-004 to AC-007/009, and REQ-005 to AC-010/013.
Acceptance rows name concrete commands, exit outcomes, static source structure, fixture output, generated files, CI order, and manifest rejection behavior.
Non-goals exclude a general shell interpreter, runtime JSON loading, token-format changes, broad lexical matching, and all authority bypasses.
The specification fixes PS5.1 XOR behavior, forced-full precedence over --lite, exact policy boundaries, immutable runner/human-copy authority, and R-10 inventory including the CI workflow.

High-risk guard, signature, evidence-path, escalation, and generation claims each have positive and negative acceptance tests; critical code-generation includes stale, poisoned-load, and manifest tamper cases.
